primary_accounting_issue: Qualification d’un actif financier au test des flux de trésorerie contractuels constitués uniquement de principal et d’intérêts (SPPI) et ses conséquences sur la catégorie de mesure IFRS 9

authority_resolution.selection_reason: I applied the strict precedence rule that IFRS supersedes IAS and selected IFRS 9 because it directly governs the classification of financial assets, including the contractual cash flow characteristics test and the resulting measurement categories.
authority_resolution.residual_uncertainty: low; the question is directly about whether contractual cash flows are limited to principal and interest, which is explicitly governed by IFRS 9 classification guidance.

primary_authority.reason: These paragraphs directly govern the SPPI assessment and the resulting IFRS 9 measurement categories for financial assets.

supporting_authority.reason: This paragraph clarifies that fair value through profit or loss can also arise by irrevocable designation to address an accounting mismatch, while remaining the same top-level measurement model.

peripheral_authority.reason: IAS 32 addresses liability-versus-equity presentation and own-equity settlement issues, not the IFRS 9 SPPI classification models for financial assets.
IFRS 15 addresses revenue recognition and some repurchase presentation issues, which are not relevant to identifying IFRS 9 classification approaches for SPPI instruments.
IAS 39 content provided here concerns hedge accounting rather than IFRS 9 financial asset classification based on SPPI.

treatment_families.family: Financial assets measured at amortised cost when the business model is to hold to collect and the contractual cash flows are solely payments of principal and interest

Financial assets measured at fair value through other comprehensive income when the business model is both collecting contractual cash flows and selling and the contractual cash flows are solely payments of principal and interest

Residual fair value measurement through profit or loss for financial assets that do not fall into the amortised cost or fair value through other comprehensive income categories, including assets failing SPPI or designated at fair value through profit or loss

approaches.label: Amortised cost
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies amortised cost as a top-level measurement category when the SPPI condition and the hold-to-collect business model are both met.

approaches.label: Fair value through other comprehensive income
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value through other comprehensive income as a top-level measurement category when the SPPI condition is met and the business model is both collecting and selling.

approaches.label: Fair value through profit or loss
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value through profit or loss as the residual top-level measurement category, including where SPPI is not met or where designation under the fair value option applies.
